description: HPE Design System token decision tree. Helps humans and AI select the correct design token by walking through intent-based questions. Start at 'root' and follow the options until you reach a 'result' node.

usage.human: Navigate the tree by answering each question. Follow the 'next' references until you reach a 'result' with token names.
usage.ai: Given a styling task, walk the tree from 'root'. At each node, select the option whose 'description' best matches the user's intent. The 'result' contains the correct token names and usage hints.

tree.question: Are you styling a specific HPE component, or looking for a general style value?

options.label: A specific component
options.description: I'm styling a known component like a Button, Select, Checkbox, etc. and want the exact component-level tokens for it.
options.hint: HPE guidance: always prefer component tokens over semantic tokens when available.

options.label: Anchor / Link
options.description: A text hyperlink, optionally with an icon.

next.question: What do you need for the Anchor?

options.label: Sizing
options.description: Font size, line height, gap between text and icon — varies by size.

result.description: Anchor sizing at xsmall. gapX is the horizontal space between the link text and an inline icon.
Medium is the default anchor size.

options.label: Appearance & states
options.description: Text color, font weight, text decoration — varies by interaction state.

result.description: Default resting state of the anchor link.

options.label: Button
options.description: Buttons of all kinds — primary, secondary, default (ghost), toolbar, CTA.

options.label: Default
options.description: A button with no fill in its resting state. Text and optional icon only.

next.question: Does the button have a text label, or is it icon-only?

result.description: Small button sizing. gapX is the space between icon and text.
Medium is the default button size.

options.label: Icon only
options.description: Icon-only buttons have equal padding and square proportions.

result.description: Icon-only buttons use equal paddingX and paddingY to form a circle/square shape.

options.label: Primary
options.description: The main call-to-action button with a solid brand-colored fill.

options.label: Secondary
options.description: A supporting action button with a visible border and no fill.

options.label: Toolbar
options.description: A compact button style used inside toolbars and action bars.

options.label: Checkbox
options.description: A checkbox with a tickable control and a label.

next.question: What part of the Checkbox are you styling?
next.hint: The 'control' is the square box you tick. The 'label' is the text beside it.

next.question: What state is the checkbox in?

result.description: The empty, unchecked checkbox at rest.
The filled, checked checkbox. iconColor is the colour of the checkmark icon.
The physical size of the checkbox box.
The horizontal gap between the checkbox square and its label text.

options.label: Switch / Toggle
options.description: A toggle switch with a sliding handle on a track.

next.question: What part of the Switch are you styling?
next.hint: The 'track' is the elongated pill background. The 'handle' is the circular knob that slides left/right.

result.description: The track background when the switch is toggled ON.
boxShadow references an elevation token that creates the raised appearance of the handle.

options.label: RadioButton
options.description: A radio button with a circular control and label.

next.question: What part of the Radio Button are you styling?
next.hint: The 'control' is the circular selector. The inner dot appears when selected.

result.description: iconColor is the colour of the inner dot/circle when selected.
Gap between the radio circle and its label.

options.label: Select / Dropdown
options.description: A dropdown selector with a trigger input, a floating option list, and individual options.

next.question: What part of the Select are you styling?
next.hint: In the HPE Design System, Select always appears inside a FormField. The trigger area is styled by FormField input container tokens. The floating dropdown ('drop') and individual options have their own Select-specific tokens. There's also a selection marker — a small left-side bar indicating focus/selection.

options.label: The trigger / control
options.description: The bordered input area you click to open the dropdown.

result.description: The Select trigger is styled by FormField input container tokens. In the HPE Design System, Select always appears inside a FormField — the border, padding, border-radius, and state styling (rest, hover, error, read-only) all come from FormField input container tokens. Navigate to FormField → 'The input container' for the full set.

options.label: The dropdown container
options.description: The outer container of the dropdown options list.

result.description: paddingX/paddingY control the inner padding of the dropdown panel. gapY is the vertical spacing between options. The dropdown itself sits inside a Drop component — use hpe.drop.default.* for the outer container background, border-radius, and z-index.

options.label: An individual option
options.description: A single row in the dropdown options list.

next.question: What do you need for the option?
What state is the option in?

options.label: The selection marker
options.description: A small vertical bar on the left edge of focused/selected options, providing a visual affordance for keyboard navigation. Typically implemented as a ::before pseudo-element.

options.label: Menu
options.description: A dropdown menu with action items, optionally grouped with separators.

next.question: What part of the Menu are you styling?
next.hint: Menus can have grouped sections of items separated by dividers. The menu itself sits inside a Drop component.

options.label: A menu item
options.description: A single clickable row in the menu list.

result.description: gapX is the space between the item's icon and text.
The container around a group of related menu items. gapY is the vertical spacing between items within the group.
The horizontal divider line between menu groups.

options.label: Drop
options.description: The generic floating container used by Select dropdowns, Menus, Tooltips, and Popovers.

next.question: What do you need for the Drop container?
next.hint: The Drop is the outer floating container. Select and Menu options sit inside a Drop. Use these tokens if you're building a custom popover, tooltip, or any floating UI element.

result.description: Background, border-radius, margin, and z-index for the floating container.

options.label: FormField
options.description: The wrapper around form inputs — provides label, border, error states, and help text.

next.question: What part of the Form Field are you styling?
next.hint: A FormField wraps inputs. The 'input container' is the bordered box around a single input (text, select). The 'input group' is for when the field wraps multiple checkboxes or radios.

options.label: The input container
options.description: The bordered box wrapping a single input — text field, Select, or similar.

result.description: The border turns to an error colour when the field has a validation error.

options.label: The input group container
options.description: When a FormField contains a CheckboxGroup or RadioButtonGroup, this is the bordered container around all the options.

options.label: An item within a group
options.description: Each individual checkbox or radio option row inside a group.

options.label: The label text
options.description: The small bold label above the input.

next.question: What do you need for the label?

result.description: The label text colour at rest.
Label text is xsmall and medium weight.

options.label: Help text
options.description: The optional secondary text displayed beneath the input field.

next.question: What do you need for the help text?

options.label: Error message text
options.description: The validation error message shown below the field.

next.question: What do you need for the error message?

options.label: Info message text
options.description: An informational message displayed below the field (non-error).

next.question: What do you need for the info message?

options.label: Placeholder text
options.description: The greyed-out hint text inside the input when it is empty.

next.question: What do you need for the placeholder?

options.label: The value text
options.description: The typed or selected value shown inside the input.

next.question: What do you need for the value text?

options.label: Data Table cells
options.description: Body cells, header cells, or footer cells in a data table.

options.label: Body cell
options.description: DataCell — the standard data rows.

options.label: Header cell
options.description: HeaderCell — the column header rows.

options.label: Footer cell
options.description: FooterCell — the summary row at the bottom.

options.label: Element
options.description: Base sizing tokens that all interactive components reference. Use these when building a custom interactive element that should align with HPE's sizing system.

result.description: paddingX has 3 variants: 'default' for standard use, 'narrow' for compact contexts, 'wide' for spacious contexts. textToIconX is the gap between text and an icon. textToElementX is the gap between text and an adjacent element.
Medium is the default element size for most interactive components.

options.label: Not sure / Not listed
options.description: My component isn't in this list, or I don't know if it has component tokens.
options.hint: Fall back to semantic tokens. They work for any component.

options.label: A general design token
options.description: I need a semantic token for color, spacing, typography, border, or sizing — not tied to a specific component.

next.question: What are you trying to style?

options.label: Color
options.description: Background fills, text colors, icon colors, border colors, foreground fills...

options.label: Background / Surface
options.description: A container, page region, or surface fill.

options.label: Page background
options.description: Elevation level 0. The backdrop containers sit on.

result.description: The overall page/app background. Creates a backdrop for containers.

options.label: Container / Card
options.description: Elevation level 1. Cards, data tables, content panels.

result.description: Lowest elevation for containers on top of the page.

options.label: Default container fill
options.description: Standard container background (white in light mode).

options.label: Floating / Overlay
options.description: Elevation level 2. Sits above everything else.

result.description: Highest elevation for floating UI: dropdowns, popovers, layers, tooltips.

options.label: Screen overlay
options.description: The dimmed backdrop rendered behind a modal or dialog.

options.label: Subtle contrast
options.description: Adds a subtle tonal shift between a region and the surface it sits on.

result.description: Use to create subtle differentiation between a component/container and the region it sits on. Has REST and hover variants.

options.label: Interactive hover/active
options.description: E.g. menu items, list items that have no fill at rest.

result.description: Generic hover/active state for components with no fill at rest.

options.label: Status communication
options.description: Backgrounds for warning, error, success, info, or unknown states.

result.description: Use for backgrounds communicating warning or caution.
Use for backgrounds communicating errors or danger.
Use for backgrounds communicating success.
Use for backgrounds communicating neutral information.
Use for backgrounds communicating unknown status.

options.label: Selected / Checked
options.description: Backgrounds for selected checkboxes, active toggles, and active tabs.

options.label: Default selected
options.description: Subtle tonal highlight for the default selected state.

options.label: Strong selected
options.description: Filled background for checked checkboxes and active toggles.

options.label: Primary / Brand emphasis
options.description: HPE green brand backgrounds for CTAs and branded elements.

options.label: Strong
options.description: Standard filled brand background for primary CTA buttons.

options.label: Extra strong
options.description: Darkest brand fill. Highest brand emphasis.

options.label: Neutral strong
options.description: Dark high-contrast backgrounds for badges and key figures.

result.description: Highest emphasis neutral palette. Dark background for badges, tags, etc.
Disabled background. Ensures disabled components are consistently styled.

options.label: Text
options.description: Labels, paragraphs, headings — text colour.

result.description: Standard readable text on default backgrounds.

options.label: Strong / emphasized
options.description: For headings, labels, and prominent text.

options.label: Weak / secondary
options.description: For captions, helper text, and metadata.

options.label: Status text
options.description: Text colors communicating status on standard backgrounds.

options.label: Text ON a colored background
options.description: Text sitting on a primary, status, or strong background — needs specific pairing for accessible contrast.

next.question: What background is the text sitting on?

options.label: Primary strong
options.description: Text on a green CTA button background.

options.label: Neutral xstrong
options.description: Text on a dark badge or high-contrast neutral background.

options.label: Selected primary
options.description: Text on a subtle selected primary background.

options.label: Icon
options.description: Icon/glyph color.

options.label: Border
options.description: Stroke colors for interactive and structural elements.

options.label: Foreground
options.description: Colors for filled UI elements — meters, progress bars, and chart regions.

options.label: Decorative
options.description: Non-semantic colors for brand elements, illustrations, and decoration.

options.label: Brand
options.description: HPE green — the primary brand color.

result.description: Use in order (10, 20, 30...) for categorical chart series. Up to 8 distinct colors.
Two-ring focus indicator. 'focus' is the primary outer ring, 'focus.support' is the inner contrasting ring.

options.label: Spacing
options.description: Gaps, padding, margins between elements.

result.description: Spacing scale for gaps, padding, and margins. Values are responsive — they change between 'large' (desktop) and 'small' (mobile) breakpoints.

options.label: Sizing
options.description: Min/max widths and heights for containers, cards, grid items.

result.description: Use for min-width, max-width, min-height, max-height of layout containers.

options.label: Typography
options.description: Font sizes, weights, line heights for headings and body text.

result.description: Medium is the default body text size.
'regular' (400) is the default. 'medium' (500) for emphasis. 'bold' (700) used sparingly.
The main font stack: 'HPE Graphik', Arial, sans-serif.

options.label: Border
options.description: Border widths (thickness) and corner radius (roundness).

result.description: 'default' is the standard border width for most uses.
'full' creates a pill/circle shape. 'medium' is common for containers.

options.label: Icon sizing
options.description: Icon dimensions (width × height).

result.description: Icon sizes. Use alongside element tokens to pair the right icon size with the right element size.
